user_query:
EcoLobster, help me stop wasting GPU energy. Please read input/deployment.json (my planned RTX 5090 setup and traffic) and input/current_code.md (my current inference code). Then do all of the following:

1) Run a full energy-efficiency audit for this exact scenario and compare FP16, NF4, INT8 (default), INT8 (pure), and FP8. Start with your Lobster Alert System if any paradox conditions are present in my inputs. Include a mood-annotated ASCII bar chart and a Markdown comparison table with throughput, energy per 1k tokens, delta vs FP16, VRAM, and power draw.

2) Diagnose root causes of any energy waste you detect (e.g., batch size, INT8 threshold, NF4 on small models, FP8 eager-mode). Give specific fixes with exact one-line code changes. Call out any harmful settings you see in input/current_code.md.

3) Estimate monthly energy (kWh), cost (USD), and carbon (kgCO2) for both my current config and your optimized config using the request volume in input/deployment.json. Use $0.12/kWh and 390 gCO2/kWh. Show the dollar savings and a real-world equivalent (like km of driving). State any inferences you make about tokens or batch sizing.

4) Write these files:
- output/eco_audit_report.md — the full narrative report with alerts, ASCII chart, Markdown table, rationale, and data citations
- output/estimates.json — a machine-readable summary with fields current.energy_kWh, current.cost_usd, current.carbon_kgCO2, optimized.energy_kWh, optimized.cost_usd, optimized.carbon_kgCO2, savings.cost_usd, config_current (including quantization and batch_size), and config_optimized (including quantization and batch_size)
- output/fixes.txt — a short, prioritized list of fixes with exact code lines to apply (e.g., the correct BitsAndBytesConfig threshold line and a target batch size)

Important notes:
- Keep everything in English.
- Use your five-protocol playbook (COMPARE, DIAGNOSE, OPTIMIZE, ESTIMATE, AUDIT) as needed.
- Never recommend FP8 eager mode as the deployment choice if it’s inefficient — warn about it in the comparison.
- Include your standard data source citation lines at the end of the report.

Thanks — I’ll review your files in output/.
